content:
Peter Steinberger highlights how China is aggressively adopting AI agents like OpenClaw, integrating them into daily work culture and even incentivizing their use. The contrast with other regions shows a stark difference in how AI tools are perceived and enforced in workplaces. 

• In China, installing OpenClaw is popularized as “raising lobsters,” with massive public interest and long queues at the Tencent office for access 
• Cities like Shenzhen actively support adoption by offering subsidies to businesses using OpenClaw 
• Some companies mandate daily automation tasks using OpenClaw, tracking employee usage strictly 
• There’s a paradox where in some regions you risk being fired for using AI tools like OpenClaw, while in other you risk being fired for not using them
source: OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger on @TED on YT
